rationale: In the literature review, the citing paper explicitly uses Naik and Raman (2003) as evidence that carry-over differs across media, even reporting the TV-versus-print ratio from that study. In the results section, it cites the same paper again to interpret its own finding that animated formats have much higher carry-over than static formats. This gives the cited paper a clear role in both framing and interpretation of a central empirical result, not just a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Naik and Raman (2003) as prior empirical grounding for heterogeneous carry-over across media, which motivates examining carry-over differences across digital ad formats. It also uses the cited study as an external benchmark to contextualize the magnitude of its Flash-versus-GIF carry-over estimates.
